body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
<!--- One sentence to describe the purpose of this PR, refer to any linked issues:
#14 -- this will link to issue 14
Closes #2 -- this will automatically close issue 2 on PR merge
-->
Closes #351 

## Content
<!---  specific tasks that are currently complete 
- Solution implemented
-->
- Use chunks, (here with `ChunkSplitters.jl` for convenience, although should be easy to get balanced chunks by hand too) 
- validated on `examples/Emulator/Ishigami` that performance is identical 1 thread and 8 threads.
- validated on unit test pass locally with `julia --project -t 8`
 
## Example - from `example/lorenz`
Replaces the loop over `1:N_ens` getting the `threadid()` each time...
```julia
nthreads = Threads.nthreads()
g_ens = zeros(nthreads, nd, N_ens)
Threads.@threads for i in 1:N_ens
    tid = Threads.threadid()
    g_ens[tid, :, i] = lorenz_forward(settings, lorenz_params[i])
end
g_ens = dropdims(sum(g_ens, dims = 1), dims = 1)
```
with with a loop that gives a chunk to each thread at the start and doesnt call `threadid()`
```julia
nthreads = Threads.nthreads()
chunked_ensemble = chunks(1:N_ens, n=nthreads) 
g_ens = zeros(nd, N_ens)
Threads.@threads for e_idx in chunked_ensemble
    for i in e_idx
        g_ens[:, i] = lorenz_forward(settings, lorenz_params[i])
    end
end
```

title: Fixing multithreading with chunks
